title: Trump’s Defense Department Sees Growing Espionage Threat From Israel
description: The Defense Department has increased the counterintelligence threat assessment to its highest level, and Israel is believed to have eavesdropped on American negotiations with Iran.
author: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt

title: Multiple People Shot Near Street Festival in Toledo, Ohio, Police Say
description: The Toledo Police Department did not provide further details about the number of people shot or the extent of their injuries.
author: Mark Walker

title: Ahead of World Cup, Two Generations of Haitian Players Unite
description: A player from Haiti’s 1974 World Cup team, the last to qualify for the tournament, met with the co-captain of this year’s squad in South Florida.
author: Patricia Mazzei

title: Senators Warn of Intelligence Gaps if Surveillance Program Expires
description: Two senior Republicans urged the Trump administration to prepare for the possible expiration of a contentious intelligence-gathering authority.
author: Robert Jimison

title: Barge Hits Maryland Rail Bridge Along Northeast Corridor
description: Amtrak said crews were conducting a safety assessment for the bridge, which crosses the Susquehanna River in Maryland. The U.S. Coast Guard reported no injuries.
author: Orlando Mayorquín

title: An Uncertain Win for Immigrants Seeking to Stay in U.S.
description: After a judge’s ruling, there was a sense of renewed hope that immigration applications that were put on hold would move forward. But how soon that would happen was unknown.
author: Jesus Jiménez

title: Kennedy Center Loses Case Against Musician Who Canceled Over Trump Naming Dispute
description: A federal judge sided with jazz performer Chuck Redd, who canceled a 2025 holiday concert after President Trump’s name was added to the building.
author: Mark Walker

title: Bob Packwood, Senator Forced to Quit in Sex Scandal, Dies at 93
description: An unorthodox Republican, he helped shape tax policy and women’s rights legislation before resigning in 1995 amid accusations by more than 20 women of sexual misconduct.
author: Katharine Q. Seelye

title: Park Ranger Dies After Falling Into a Crevasse on Mt. McKinley
description: Robin Pendery, 33, who was part of a climbing patrol, was near a camp at about 14,000 feet on the mountain in Alaska when she fell, the National Park Service said.
author: Neil Vigdor

title: C.I.A. Officer Found With Gold Bars Said to Have Created Fake Spy Program
description: A federal judge has ruled that David Rush, the C.I.A. employee, must remain in detention. U.S. officials say Mr. Rush funneled millions in federal funds to himself.
author: Julian E. Barnes and Edward Wong

title: YouTuber and Wife Ended Pregnancy After Down Syndrome Diagnosis. They Got Death Threats.
description: After Jesse Ridgway, a popular YouTuber, and his wife, Ashley, revealed the news, death threats followed.
author: Pooja Salhotra

title: Paxton’s Senate Bid Raises the Stakes in His War on Latino Voting Groups
description: The Texas attorney general has mounted an all-out effort to prove Democratic Hispanic groups have been corrupting elections. Now he could be the beneficiary of his own attacks.
author: Jazmine Ulloa and Edgar Sandoval

title: The Bidens Return With New Book and South Dakota Speech Targeting Trump
description: The re-emergence of the former first family has been fraught for some Democrats who just want to move on.
author: Shawn McCreesh

title: Trump Greets Farmers in Wisconsin, but Says He Could Be Home Watching TV
description: President Trump was in Wisconsin to reassure farmers who have been stung by his tariff policies and rising fuel prices from the war in Iran.
author: Katie Rogers

title: Deluged With Mail Ballots, California Takes Its Time Counting Votes Again
description: Experts say speeding up the count in California would take more resources, but also scaling back rules that expand voting access.
author: Laurel Rosenhall, Jill Cowan and Livia Albeck-Ripka

title: Dallas’s Halperin Park Seeks to Reconnect Oak Cliff Decades After Highway Divided Neighborhood
description: Built atop a freeway that once split a historic Black neighborhood, Halperin Park attempts to reconnect communities divided by midcentury highway construction.
author: Jesus Jiménez and Shelby Tauber

title: As Trump Pushes Deportations, a Skyrocketing Caseload Strains Immigration Courts
description: A federal surge has more than doubled caseloads within some immigration courts nationwide. Lawyers say the tactic is causing errors and confusion.
author: Jazmine Ulloa and Hamed Aleaziz

title: Trump Pardons Stephen Buyer, Former Congressman Convicted of Insider Trading
description: Stephen Buyer, a former Republican representative from Indiana, was convicted of trading stock related to two deals before they were made public.
author: Francesca Regalado

title: Graham Platner Says His Past Is Being ‘Weaponized’ Amid Growing Democratic Concern
description: The presumptive Democratic Senate nominee in Maine said the state would have his back in the face of accusations he has denied.
author: Tim Balk and Katie Glueck

title: Trump Says Bill Pulte, His New Intelligence Director, Should Slash Staff
description: The president suggested that employees who worked for previous Democratic presidents were among those who should be fired.
author: Maggie Haberman
